title: Property Dispute - Ancestral Land Partition
description: A dispute arose in 2024 between the Sharma siblings over partition of ancestral land located in Lucknow, originally owned by their late father, Raghunath Sharma. After his death in 2018, no formal partition was executed. The eldest son, Amit Sharma, has been in possession of the majority of the land and claims an oral family settlement granting him a larger share due to financial contributions. The other siblings, Neha Sharma and Rohit Sharma, contest this claim and seek equal division as per succession laws.

characters.name: Amit Sharma
characters.details: Eldest son, in possession of major portion, claims oral family settlement

characters.name: Neha Sharma
characters.details: Daughter seeking equal share in ancestral property

characters.name: Rohit Sharma
characters.details: Younger son supporting equal division claim

characters.name: Late Raghunath Sharma
characters.details: Original owner of the ancestral property

characters.name: Suresh Verma
characters.details: Neighbor claiming knowledge of alleged oral family settlement

characters.name: Anita Gupta
characters.details: Government-approved surveyor who prepared land division report

evidence: Property title documents in name of Raghunath Sharma
Survey report showing current possession and boundaries
Electricity and utility bills in Amit Sharma's name
Witness statement regarding oral family settlement
evidence_chain: Property originally owned by deceased father
No registered partition executed after death
All legal heirs entitled under succession law
Defendant claims larger share via oral settlement
Possession and utility records support defendant’s control
Plaintiffs challenge validity of oral agreement
Court must determine enforceability of oral partition vs statutory rights
legal_ambiguity: Whether an unregistered oral family settlement can override statutory equal inheritance rights and how possession and contribution impact partition.
